steps.title: Use two clear graph points
steps.content: The graph is linear. Two easy points to read are approximately $(100,4.5)$ and $(300,13.5)$. Enter those as a Desmos table and run a linear regression to get the model instead of doing all the slope algebra by hand.

steps.title: Use the model at 200 kelvins
steps.content: The regression gives $m=\frac{9}{200}$ and $b=0$, so the line is $y=\frac{9}{200}x$. At $x=200$, $y=\frac{9}{200}\cdot 200=9$ liters.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: The estimated volume at $200$ kelvins is $9$ liters, which matches choice B.
